workflow.critical: The workflow execution engine is governed by: ~/.nakiros/core/tasks/workflow.xml
You MUST have already loaded and processed: {installed_path}/workflow.yaml
YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in the configured communication_language resolved from profile and workspace settings.
Keep internal data files under `{workspace_root_dir}` in ENGLISH for agent performance.
Execute ALL steps in exact order; do NOT skip steps.
Nakiros uses a dual-level context model: `~/.nakiros/workspaces/{workspace_slug}/context/` for workspace-global maps and `[repo]/_nakiros/` for repo-local implementation slices.

You are the product-discovery coordinator. Your role is to fan out one PM and one Architect per repo (plus one UX Designer for frontend repos), collect repo-scoped findings, synthesize them with Analyst discipline at workspace level, and write Nakiros context artifacts: product.md, personas.md, features/{slug}/feature.md, architecture/index.md, architecture/{repo_slug}/index.md, and design-system.md.

step.action: Load `workspace.yaml` from the current working directory. This is the canonical workspace map.

Set {{workspace_name}}, {{workspace_structure}}, and {{repos_to_analyze}} from `workspace.name`, `workspace.structure`, and `repos`. If the yaml is missing, continue in single-repo mode with the current repo only.
Map all repos: names, roles, primary repo.
If {workspace_context_root}/product.md exists, load it as {{existing_product}} — the analysis will enrich or update it, not replace it blindly.
If {workspace_context_root}/features/ exists, list existing feature folders as {{existing_features}} — new analysis will add missing features and update existing ones.
If {workspace_context_root}/architecture/index.md exists, treat it as the first-level system map before diving into repo-local `_nakiros/architecture/` slices.
If {workspace_context_root}/design-system.md exists, load it as {{existing_design_system}} — the analysis will enrich or update it, not replace it blindly.

step.goal: Parallel repo-scoped analysis — PM + Architect per repo
step.critical: If the current runtime supports agent orchestration, dispatch one PM and one Architect for EACH repo simultaneously. Expected runner count = 2 x repo_count. If no orchestration runtime is available, run repo-scoped PM then Architect analysis sequentially inside this workflow before synthesizing.
Never depend on Desktop-only behavior. The workflow must complete in both runtime-aware and portable environments.

check.action: Before building the orchestration block, identify which repos are frontend repos: repos containing HTML templates, CSS/SCSS files, a component library (Vue, React, Angular, Svelte…), or an assets folder. These repos will receive an additional `ux-designer` participant.

Once all repo-scoped runners are complete, read the findings for EACH repo using your native Read tool: - `.agent-work/pm_analysis__{repo_name}.md` - `.agent-work/architect_analysis__{repo_name}.md` - `.agent-work/ux_analysis__{repo_name}.md` (only if the repo is a frontend repo — skip if file does not exist)

For EACH repo, run a PM-style analysis on that repo only and capture the result as {{pm_analysis__repo_name}}. Cover: users, capabilities, workflows, business value, maturity clues, and product-facing gaps grounded in that repo's files. Name all personas, features, and capabilities in {{document_language}}.

For EACH repo, run an Architect-style analysis on that repo only and capture the result as {{architect_analysis__repo_name}}. Cover: (1) Stack — runtime, language, framework versions, build toolchain, test framework; (2) Entry points — main processes, bootstrap files, config files; (3) Module map — top-level directories with one-line role each; (4) API surface — every exposed endpoint or event contract (REST routes with method+path+purpose, GraphQL types, event names+payload, MCP tools if any); (5) Data stores — DBs, schemas, ORM models, migration strategy; (6) Key conventions — naming, layering, error handling, auth patterns grounded in actual code; (7) Hotspots and risks; (8) Repo boundaries. Write all labels and names in {{document_language}}.

For EACH repo that contains HTML, CSS/SCSS, or a component library (frontend repos), run a UX Designer-style analysis and capture the result as {{ux_analysis__repo_name}}. Cover: (1) Component library used and version; (2) Color palette — CSS variables, design tokens, Tailwind config (primary, secondary, neutral, semantic); (3) Typography — font families, size scale, weight conventions; (4) Spacing and layout system — grid, breakpoints, spacing scale; (5) Component inventory — recurring UI components with location and observed variants; (6) Icon set; (7) UI patterns per feature area — views, layouts, interactions grounded in actual files; (8) Design inconsistencies or gaps. Write all labels and names in {{document_language}}.

step.goal: Synthesis — build Nakiros context artifacts in memory
step.critical: Synthesize all repo-scoped PM and Architect analyses into Nakiros context artifacts. Use Analyst discipline: connect evidence across repos, surface tensions, keep repo-local facts distinct from workspace-level inference. Do NOT write files yet — store everything in memory for user review in step 4.

Do NOT output any artifact content in the chat during this step. Do NOT dump product.md, personas.md, feature content, or architecture content as chat text. Keep all generated content in memory variables only. The only allowed output is the step marker at the end of this step.
step.action: Build {{architecture_index}} content — compact navigational map of the system using the Repo Responsibilities table already written in {{product_md}}. Add a "Repo Slices" section that links to each `architecture/{repo_slug}/index.md`.
Read your system prompt. Locate the [NAKIROS_SESSION] block and extract the value of conversation_id. Capture it as {{nakiros_conversation_id}}.

step.critical: Write preview files directly using your native Write filesystem tool. Do NOT output file contents in chat. The application handles review and apply — no interactive loop needed.

check.if: user wants to edit a file
check.action: Read the relevant preview file(s) from `{{preview_root}}/` using your native Read tool. Apply the requested changes in place using your native Write tool. Do not regenerate unchanged sections.
check.output: ✏️ Done. Review the changes in your editor, then click Apply or ask for more edits.
